query: Can I get my student confirmations myself, or do I have to request them?

queries: Can I get my student confirmations myself, or do I have to request them?

retrieved.title: Confirmations for third parties
retrieved.text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

retrieved.title: Confirmations
retrieved.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. For confirmations issued in English you first switch the language u:space is displayed in to English. These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
The payment confirmation for the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents > Semester specific documents > payment request/confirmation.

A confirmation of the payment of the administration fee for [bachelor programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) is included in the registration confirmation for the entrance exam procedure. This document is available when you have applied for admission to one of these programmes.

Your examination results are included in your transcript of records and the confirmation of courses passed in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en).

If you need confirmations beyond that or if you have no access to u:space anymore please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a duplicate of your degree certificate or diploma supplement please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a confirmation of your academic degree for an external agency please contact the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) at [verifications@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,xgtkhkecvkqpuBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Exempt from this are degrees from dental or medical studies.

If you have studied dental or human medicine in the past please contact the [Medical University of Vienna](https://www.meduniwien.ac.at/web/en/) directly. Since 1 January 2004 it is no longer part of the University of Vienna. Your data is available at the Medical University and can be verified there.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: The confirmation of study periods is one of the so-called study documents. It contains information about your periods of study at the University of Vienna. As long as you are admitted to a degree programme, you can find your confirmation of study periods in u:space under "Personal Matters" > "My documents". If your u:account is no longer active, you can request the confirmation of study periods. Related Links: - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast) - [Confirmations for former students](/en/confirmations/) Related entries: - [Printing service](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30816&cHash=22367dd134bbef04eb88dbbc67702909) - [Confirmation of enrolment ("Studienbestätigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31270&cHash=2d54c226eb7b6b7ca5875bf23578fd10) - [Student record sheet ("Studienblatt")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31272&cHash=1648f92d42d01e53f091d5bb41e42299) - [Study documents](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31288&cHash=96775fee4d128222ffe4a11102a051f9) - Confirmation of withdrawal ("Abgangsbescheinigung") The confirmation of withdrawal is used as a confirmation of your requested withdrawal from a degree programme (for successfully completed degree programmes you obtain your final documents from your StudiesServiceCenter). You can print your confirmation of withdrawal via u:space by clicking Personal Matters" > "My documents". Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) and StudiesServiceUnits (‘StudienServiceStelle’, SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) Related entries: - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31292&cHash=8fb756fa18f12c081793a7bcf39b7390) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Continuation of studies Related entries: - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - Course (LV) To take exams, you have to attend courses ("Lehrveranstaltung", LV). There are courses with continuous assessment and courses with non-continuous assessment. In the first case, you have to deliver results already during the semester. Courses with non-continuous assessment end with an examination at the end of the semester resp. in

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

retrieved.title: Confirmations
retrieved.text: - Confirmation of study periods In your confirmation of study periods you find an overview of all degree and non-degree programmes you are or were admitted to at the University of Vienna. It contains data regarding the beginning and end of studies, re-registered semesters and semesters on leave. The confirmation of study periods is available in German and English. - Confirmation of enrolment Your confirmation of enrolment confirms that you are enrolled for a specific degree or non-degree programme in a specific semester. If you are admitted to multiple programmes at the University of Vienna there is a separate confirmation of enrolment for each programme. Other authorities often refer to the confirmation of enrolment as "Inskriptionsbestätigung". The confirmation of enrolment is available in German and English. You can download the document for the current semester as soon as the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee has been received by the University of Vienna. - Student record sheet In your student record sheet you can find an overview of all programmes you are and were admitted to. It is issued for each semester. The student record sheet contains information about the curriculum and version, beginning of studies, enrolment status (or end of studies) and possible resumptions of studies. The student record sheet is available in German and English. You can download the document for the current semester as soon as the prescribed

retrieved.title: Confirmations for third parties
heading_path: Verifications of education or academic degree
retrieved.text:
Due to data protection regulations you have to keep several things in mind for the verification of academic degrees.

The Büro Studienpräses needs a scan of the personal ID of the person (as JPEG or PDF) as well as a signed declaration of consent. This declaration has to include the following information:

- Specification of the data required (e.g. academic degree, number of achieved ECTS etc.)
- Name
- Date of birth
- Address of the office or name and address of the person concerned
- Degree programme
- Student ID number
- Purpose

Send your request for verification of academic degrees to the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) at [verifications@univie.ac.at](mailto:verifications@univie.ac.at). An exemption are degrees of dental and medical degree programmes.

For confirmations of degrees of dental and medical programmes please contact the [Medical University of Vienna](https://www.meduniwien.ac.at/web/en/), even if the diploma was issued by the University of Vienna (this was the case until 2004).

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: soon as your tuition fee contribution appears in u:space as "received on...", you can print out your student record sheet and your confirmation of enrolment for the respective semester. Related Links: - [u:space](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273319) Related entries: - [Confirmation of courses passed (‘Bestätigung über positiv absolvierte Prüfungen’)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30833&cHash=384abc77d3971dc5719c107fd6ea13f4) - [Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31251&cHash=96a657912ac9ab41a08745ded22bb839) - [Confirmation of enrolment ("Studienbestätigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31270&cHash=2d54c226eb7b6b7ca5875bf23578fd10) - [Student record sheet ("Studienblatt")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31272&cHash=1648f92d42d01e53f091d5bb41e42299) - [Proof of academic success ("Studienerfolgsnachweis")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31276&cHash=67d9bdbca177eb0e3f60926ca3882521) - [Confirmation of study periods ("Studienzeitbestätigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31294&cHash=be04add5fe3411a86e9b4c39c5b95416) - Study law You have rights and obligations that govern your studies from admission until graduation. If you have any questions, you can contact the following offices: - The Directorate of Studies (SPL) is responsible for your degree programme and should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and administrative enquiries during your studies. - Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of alternative modes of assessment for students with impairments. The SPL takes the fundamental decisions about this. The Accessible Studying team (‘Team Barrierefrei’) offers (also anonymous) advice. - The Office of the Studienpräses provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the SPL can solve. - If you have questions about admission and the tuition fee, please contact the Admission Office. - In individual cases, the ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende') of the Federal Ministry can help

retrieved.title: Education Fairs
retrieved.text:
[## You've got further questions?](/en/contact-form/)

[Choosing the topic "degree programme and admission in general" or "Schule trifft Uni" in the contact form is the best way to reach the responsible office.](/en/contact-form/)

retrieved.title: Alternative mode of assessment
retrieved.text:
If your application is granted or if you received a “letter of recommendation” (issued until February 2020), your teachers/examiners are not automatically notified and you are not automatically registered in the relevant university system.

A granted application merely confirms your right to the alternative mode of assessment and provides a solid basis for communication. You have to independently contact your lecturers/examiners in all your courses/exams and inform them that you require an alternative mode of assessment. This is also due to reasons of data protection. Furthermore, some impairments occur in episodes or phases. Therefore, affected students might not have the same needs at all times. An automated system could not take this into account.

Therefore, contact your lecturer or examiner as soon as possible to discuss your needs.

According to section 59, para. 1, item 12 of the [2002 Universities Act](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128) (in German), degree programme students and non-degree programme students with an [impairment that directly affects their studies](/en/accessible-studies/) have the right to alternative modes of assessment.

In addition, the Statutes of the University of Vienna specify that there are [equal opportunities for people with a disability and/or a chronic illness](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/more-parts-of-the-statutes/equal-opportunities-for-people-with-disabilities-andor-chronic-illnesses/).

You can find the detailed legal texts on the [Accessible University of Vienna portal](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/weiterfuehrend/rechtliches-und-nachweise/pruefungen/) (in German).

retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
retrieved.text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. Related Links: - [Registration overview in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/anmeldeuebersicht) - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Registration deadline for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30189&cHash=de0ff6f2f9646d32b8000b74fd539a40) - [Registration for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30193&cHash=7fbe81eeddcfae3bd66265dbbd660f93) - [Registration system for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Course with non-continuous assessment (npi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31201&cHash=60e218c8de231c88ec12793220a06022) - [Allocation of places in courses and

retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: for confirming your pre-approval. - Where can I find the student record sheet of the University of Vienna?? You can download your student record sheet as soon as the co-registration has been approved. You can find the student record sheet in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents. - What information can I find on the student record sheet? The student record sheet confirms the current status of all your studies at the University of Vienna. As you are co-registered at the University of Vienna, the degree programme code and the code letter of your home university will appear on the student record sheet. - Do I receive a library card when I co-register? Your u:card is your [ID card for the University Library](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/bibliotheksausweis.html). You can order it in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My u:card.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

reranked.title: Confirmations
reranked.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. For confirmations issued in English you first switch the language u:space is displayed in to English. These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

reranked.title: Confirmations for (former) students
reranked.text:
The payment confirmation for the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents > Semester specific documents > payment request/confirmation.

A confirmation of the payment of the administration fee for [bachelor programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) is included in the registration confirmation for the entrance exam procedure. This document is available when you have applied for admission to one of these programmes.

Your examination results are included in your transcript of records and the confirmation of courses passed in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en).

If you need confirmations beyond that or if you have no access to u:space anymore please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a duplicate of your degree certificate or diploma supplement please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a confirmation of your academic degree for an external agency please contact the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) at [verifications@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,xgtkhkecvkqpuBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Exempt from this are degrees from dental or medical studies.

If you have studied dental or human medicine in the past please contact the [Medical University of Vienna](https://www.meduniwien.ac.at/web/en/) directly. Since 1 January 2004 it is no longer part of the University of Vienna. Your data is available at the Medical University and can be verified there.

The payment request/confirmation is available as bilingual document (German and English). - Confirmation of withdrawal If you close one (or multiple) of your degree programmes you can download a confirmation of withdrawal for this programme. For successfully completed programmes a confirmation of

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: The confirmation of study periods is one of the so-called study documents. It contains information about your periods of study at the University of Vienna. As long as you are admitted to a degree programme, you can find your confirmation of study periods in u:space under "Personal Matters" > "My documents". If your u:account is no longer active, you can request the confirmation of study periods. Related Links: - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast) - [Confirmations for former students](/en/confirmations/) Related entries: - [Printing service](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30816&cHash=22367dd134bbef04eb88dbbc67702909) - [Confirmation of enrolment ("Studienbestätigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31270&cHash=2d54c226eb7b6b7ca5875bf23578fd10) - [Student record sheet ("Studienblatt")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31272&cHash=1648f92d42d01e53f091d5bb41e42299) - [Study documents](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31288&cHash=96775fee4d128222ffe4a11102a051f9) - Confirmation of withdrawal ("Abgangsbescheinigung") The confirmation of withdrawal is used as a confirmation of your requested withdrawal from a degree programme (for successfully completed degree programmes you obtain your final documents from your StudiesServiceCenter). You can print your confirmation of withdrawal via u:space by clicking Personal Matters" > "My documents". Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) and StudiesServiceUnits (‘StudienServiceStelle’, SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) Related entries: - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31292&cHash=8fb756fa18f12c081793a7bcf39b7390) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Continuation of studies Related entries: - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - Course (LV) To take exams, you have to attend courses ("Lehrveranstaltung", LV). There are courses with continuous assessment and courses with non-continuous assessment. In the first case, you have to deliver results already during the semester. Courses with non-continuous assessment end with an examination at the end of the semester resp. in

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

reranked.title: Confirmations
reranked.text: - Confirmation of study periods In your confirmation of study periods you find an overview of all degree and non-degree programmes you are or were admitted to at the University of Vienna. It contains data regarding the beginning and end of studies, re-registered semesters and semesters on leave. The confirmation of study periods is available in German and English. - Confirmation of enrolment Your confirmation of enrolment confirms that you are enrolled for a specific degree or non-degree programme in a specific semester. If you are admitted to multiple programmes at the University of Vienna there is a separate confirmation of enrolment for each programme. Other authorities often refer to the confirmation of enrolment as "Inskriptionsbestätigung". The confirmation of enrolment is available in German and English. You can download the document for the current semester as soon as the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee has been received by the University of Vienna. - Student record sheet In your student record sheet you can find an overview of all programmes you are and were admitted to. It is issued for each semester. The student record sheet contains information about the curriculum and version, beginning of studies, enrolment status (or end of studies) and possible resumptions of studies. The student record sheet is available in German and English. You can download the document for the current semester as soon as the prescribed

reranked.title: Confirmations for third parties
heading_path: Verifications of education or academic degree
reranked.text:
Due to data protection regulations you have to keep several things in mind for the verification of academic degrees.

The Büro Studienpräses needs a scan of the personal ID of the person (as JPEG or PDF) as well as a signed declaration of consent. This declaration has to include the following information:

- Specification of the data required (e.g. academic degree, number of achieved ECTS etc.)
- Name
- Date of birth
- Address of the office or name and address of the person concerned
- Degree programme
- Student ID number
- Purpose

Send your request for verification of academic degrees to the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) at [verifications@univie.ac.at](mailto:verifications@univie.ac.at). An exemption are degrees of dental and medical degree programmes.

For confirmations of degrees of dental and medical programmes please contact the [Medical University of Vienna](https://www.meduniwien.ac.at/web/en/), even if the diploma was issued by the University of Vienna (this was the case until 2004).

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: soon as your tuition fee contribution appears in u:space as "received on...", you can print out your student record sheet and your confirmation of enrolment for the respective semester. Related Links: - [u:space](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273319) Related entries: - [Confirmation of courses passed (‘Bestätigung über positiv absolvierte Prüfungen’)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30833&cHash=384abc77d3971dc5719c107fd6ea13f4) - [Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31251&cHash=96a657912ac9ab41a08745ded22bb839) - [Confirmation of enrolment ("Studienbestätigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31270&cHash=2d54c226eb7b6b7ca5875bf23578fd10) - [Student record sheet ("Studienblatt")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31272&cHash=1648f92d42d01e53f091d5bb41e42299) - [Proof of academic success ("Studienerfolgsnachweis")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31276&cHash=67d9bdbca177eb0e3f60926ca3882521) - [Confirmation of study periods ("Studienzeitbestätigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31294&cHash=be04add5fe3411a86e9b4c39c5b95416) - Study law You have rights and obligations that govern your studies from admission until graduation. If you have any questions, you can contact the following offices: - The Directorate of Studies (SPL) is responsible for your degree programme and should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and administrative enquiries during your studies. - Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of alternative modes of assessment for students with impairments. The SPL takes the fundamental decisions about this. The Accessible Studying team (‘Team Barrierefrei’) offers (also anonymous) advice. - The Office of the Studienpräses provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the SPL can solve. - If you have questions about admission and the tuition fee, please contact the Admission Office. - In individual cases, the ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende') of the Federal Ministry can help

reranked.title: Education Fairs
reranked.text:
[## You've got further questions?](/en/contact-form/)

[Choosing the topic "degree programme and admission in general" or "Schule trifft Uni" in the contact form is the best way to reach the responsible office.](/en/contact-form/)

reranked.title: Alternative mode of assessment
reranked.text:
If your application is granted or if you received a “letter of recommendation” (issued until February 2020), your teachers/examiners are not automatically notified and you are not automatically registered in the relevant university system.

A granted application merely confirms your right to the alternative mode of assessment and provides a solid basis for communication. You have to independently contact your lecturers/examiners in all your courses/exams and inform them that you require an alternative mode of assessment. This is also due to reasons of data protection. Furthermore, some impairments occur in episodes or phases. Therefore, affected students might not have the same needs at all times. An automated system could not take this into account.

Therefore, contact your lecturer or examiner as soon as possible to discuss your needs.

According to section 59, para. 1, item 12 of the [2002 Universities Act](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128) (in German), degree programme students and non-degree programme students with an [impairment that directly affects their studies](/en/accessible-studies/) have the right to alternative modes of assessment.

In addition, the Statutes of the University of Vienna specify that there are [equal opportunities for people with a disability and/or a chronic illness](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/more-parts-of-the-statutes/equal-opportunities-for-people-with-disabilities-andor-chronic-illnesses/).

You can find the detailed legal texts on the [Accessible University of Vienna portal](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/weiterfuehrend/rechtliches-und-nachweise/pruefungen/) (in German).

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
reranked.text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

reranked.title: Confirmations for (former) students
reranked.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. Related Links: - [Registration overview in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/anmeldeuebersicht) - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Registration deadline for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30189&cHash=de0ff6f2f9646d32b8000b74fd539a40) - [Registration for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30193&cHash=7fbe81eeddcfae3bd66265dbbd660f93) - [Registration system for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Course with non-continuous assessment (npi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31201&cHash=60e218c8de231c88ec12793220a06022) - [Allocation of places in courses and

reranked.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: for confirming your pre-approval. - Where can I find the student record sheet of the University of Vienna?? You can download your student record sheet as soon as the co-registration has been approved. You can find the student record sheet in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents. - What information can I find on the student record sheet? The student record sheet confirms the current status of all your studies at the University of Vienna. As you are co-registered at the University of Vienna, the degree programme code and the code letter of your home university will appear on the student record sheet. - Do I receive a library card when I co-register? Your u:card is your [ID card for the University Library](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/bibliotheksausweis.html). You can order it in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My u:card.

selected.title: Confirmations for third parties
selected.text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

selected.title: Confirmations for (former) students
selected.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

selected.title: Confirmations for (former) students
selected.text:
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

This document is available when you have applied for admission to one of these programmes.

Your examination results are included in your transcript of records and the confirmation of courses passed in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en).

If you need confirmations beyond that or if you have no access to u:space anymore please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a confirmation of your academic degree for an external agency please contact the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) at [verifications@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,xgtkhkecvkqpuBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Exempt from this are degrees from dental or medical studies.

If you have studied dental or human medicine in the past please contact the [Medical University of Vienna](https://www.meduniwien.ac.at/web/en/) directly. Since 1 January 2004 it is no longer part of the University of Vienna. Your data is available at the Medical University and can be verified there.

Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

[2] Confirmations for (former) students
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

[3] Confirmations for (former) students
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

[4] Confirmations
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. For confirmations issued in English you first switch the language u:space is displayed in to English. These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

[5] Confirmations for (former) students
The payment confirmation for the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents > Semester specific documents > payment request/confirmation.

A confirmation of the payment of the administration fee for [bachelor programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) is included in the registration confirmation for the entrance exam procedure. This document is available when you have applied for admission to one of these programmes.

Your examination results are included in your transcript of records and the confirmation of courses passed in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en).

If you need confirmations beyond that or if you have no access to u:space anymore please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a duplicate of your degree certificate or diploma supplement please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en) of your degree programme.

If you need a confirmation of your academic degree for an external agency please contact the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) at [verifications@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,xgtkhkecvkqpuBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Exempt from this are degrees from dental or medical studies.

If you have studied dental or human medicine in the past please contact the [Medical University of Vienna](https://www.meduniwien.ac.at/web/en/) directly. Since 1 January 2004 it is no longer part of the University of Vienna. Your data is available at the Medical University and can be verified there.

[6] Confirmations
[### Confirmations for (former) students](/en/confirmations-students/)

[### Confirmations for third parties](/en/confirmations-third-parties/)

- [Information about confirmations for employees (login required)](https://wiki.univie.ac.at/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=363964279)
- [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en)

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Confirmations for third parties
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Question: Can I get my student confirmations myself, or do I have to request them?

generate.raw_response: You can download most confirmations yourself in u:space at Personal matters > My documents [1][2][4]. However, if you need special confirmations or those with a "wet" stamp and signature, you need to request them via the Servicedesk [2] or contact the relevant office [1][3][5].

answer: You can download most confirmations yourself in u:space at Personal matters > My documents [1][2][4]. However, if you need special confirmations or those with a "wet" stamp and signature, you need to request them via the Servicedesk [2] or contact the relevant office [1][3][5].
